flashcards.q: What are congruent figures?
flashcards.a: Figures that have the same shape and size and can be superimposed exactly one over the other.

flashcards.q: Can a figure be rotated or flipped when checking for congruence?
flashcards.a: Yes. Rotation and flipping are allowed — they do not change shape or size.

flashcards.q: What does the symbol ≅ mean?
flashcards.a: It means 'is congruent to'. Example: ΔABC ≅ ΔXYZ.

flashcards.q: In ΔABC ≅ ΔXYZ, which vertex corresponds to B?
flashcards.a: Y. The order of vertices in the congruence statement defines the correspondence: A↔X, B↔Y, C↔Z.

flashcards.q: What is the SSS condition for congruence?
flashcards.a: If all three sides of one triangle equal the corresponding three sides of another, the triangles are congruent (Side-Side-Side).

flashcards.q: What is the SAS condition for congruence?
flashcards.a: Two sides and the included angle (angle between those sides) of one triangle equal the corresponding parts of another (Side-Angle-Side).

flashcards.q: What is the included angle?
flashcards.a: The angle formed between two specific sides of a triangle. E.g., ∠B is the included angle between sides AB and BC.

flashcards.q: What is the included side?
flashcards.a: The side lying between two specific angles of a triangle. E.g., BC is the included side between ∠B and ∠C.

flashcards.q: What is the ASA condition for congruence?
flashcards.a: Two angles and the included side of one triangle equal the corresponding two angles and included side of another (Angle-Side-Angle).

flashcards.q: What is the AAS condition for congruence?
flashcards.a: Two angles and a non-included side of one triangle equal the corresponding parts of another (Angle-Angle-Side). It guarantees congruence.

flashcards.q: Why does AAS guarantee congruence even though the side is not between the angles?
flashcards.a: Because the third angle is fixed by the angle sum property (180°), converting AAS into an equivalent ASA case.

flashcards.q: What is the RHS condition for congruence?
flashcards.a: The hypotenuse and one leg of a right-angled triangle equal the hypotenuse and corresponding leg of another right-angled triangle (Right angle-Hypotenuse-Side).

flashcards.q: What is the hypotenuse of a right-angled triangle?
flashcards.a: The side opposite to the right angle (90°). It is always the longest side of the right-angled triangle.

flashcards.q: Does the SSA condition guarantee congruence?
flashcards.a: No. Two non-congruent triangles can have the same two sides and non-included angle, so SSA does not guarantee congruence.

flashcards.q: Does the AAA condition guarantee congruence?
flashcards.a: No. Triangles with all three angles equal may have different sizes (they are similar, not necessarily congruent).

flashcards.q: List the five conditions that guarantee triangle congruence.
flashcards.a: SSS, SAS, ASA, AAS, and RHS.

flashcards.q: True or False: Two triangles with angles 40°, 60°, 80° must be congruent.
flashcards.a: False. They have the same shape (similar) but may have different side lengths, so they need not be congruent.

flashcards.q: True or False: If AB = PQ, BC = QR, and ∠B = ∠Q, then ΔABC ≅ ΔPQR.
flashcards.a: True — by the SAS condition (∠B is the included angle between AB and BC; ∠Q is the included angle between PQ and QR).

flashcards.q: In an isosceles triangle, which angles are equal?
flashcards.a: The angles opposite the two equal sides (the base angles) are equal.

flashcards.q: State the isosceles triangle theorem.
flashcards.a: In a triangle, angles opposite to equal sides are equal. If AB = AC in ΔABC, then ∠B = ∠C.

flashcards.q: What are the angles of an equilateral triangle?
flashcards.a: All three angles are equal and each measures 60°.

flashcards.q: How is the equilateral triangle angle property proved using congruence?
flashcards.a: All sides are equal → by the isosceles property, all angles are equal → since angles sum to 180°, each angle = 60°.

flashcards.q: In isosceles ΔABC with AB = AC and ∠A = 80°, find ∠B.
What does it mean for two triangles to have 'corresponding parts'?
flashcards.a: Parts (vertices, sides, angles) that overlap exactly when the congruent triangles are superimposed.

flashcards.q: If ΔABC ≅ ΔPQR, which side equals BC?
flashcards.a: QR (B↔Q, C↔R, so BC corresponds to QR).

flashcards.q: Is ΔABC ≅ ΔBCA a valid way to express congruence of the same triangle with itself?
flashcards.a: Only if A↔B, B↔C, C↔A makes equal sides overlap — this is valid for equilateral triangles but not for scalene ones.

flashcards.q: In a right triangle, which condition uses both the hypotenuse and a leg?
flashcards.a: RHS (Right angle-Hypotenuse-Side).

flashcards.q: Why is RHS a valid congruence condition even though it looks like SSA?
flashcards.a: In RHS, the right angle fixes one angle, and having the hypotenuse (the longest side) opposite to it eliminates the ambiguity that makes general SSA fail.

flashcards.q: True or False: If two triangles are congruent by SSS, their corresponding angles are also equal.
flashcards.a: True. When two triangles are congruent by any condition, ALL corresponding sides AND angles are equal.

flashcards.q: What is the minimum information needed to check if two circles are congruent?
flashcards.a: Their radii must be equal — two circles with equal radii are congruent.

flashcards.q: What is the minimum information needed to check if two rectangles are congruent?
flashcards.a: Both the length and the breadth must respectively be equal.

flashcards.q: In ΔABC with AB = AC, the altitude from A meets BC at D. What can be said about BD and DC?
flashcards.a: BD = DC. The altitude from the apex of an isosceles triangle bisects the base (proved by RHS congruence of ΔADB and ΔADC).

flashcards.q: Two congruent figures — can one always be superimposed on the other without flipping?
flashcards.a: Not always. Sometimes a flip (reflection) is needed, for example in mirror-image triangles. Flipping is permitted when checking congruence.

flashcards.q: If ΔABC ≅ ΔXYZ, write one other correct congruence statement for the same pair.
flashcards.a: ΔBCA ≅ ΔYZX (or any cyclic rotation maintaining the same vertex correspondence A↔X, B↔Y, C↔Z).

flashcards.q: True or False: SSA guarantees congruence when the side opposite the angle is longer than the other given side.
flashcards.a: True. In that special case the arc can intersect the line at only one valid point, making the triangle unique and congruent.
